1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church of the Thessalonians, who belong to God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: May grace and peace be yours.
We always thank God for all of you, remembering you in our prayers.
We never stop remembering how your faith leads you to work, how your love makes you serve, and how your hope in our Lord Jesus Christ keeps you going. We remember this before our God and Father.
Brothers and sisters, God loves you, and we know that he has chosen you.
For when we brought you the good news, it did not come only in words. It came with power, with the Holy Spirit, and with deep certainty. You know how we lived among you, for your good.
And you became like us and like the Lord. Even though you suffered greatly, you welcomed the message with the joy that the Holy Spirit gives.
So you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
The Lord's message rang out from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia. Your faith in God has become known everywhere, so that we do not even need to say anything.
People themselves tell us how you welcomed us, and how you turned away from idols to serve the living and true God,
and to wait for his Son from heaven — Jesus, whom God raised from death, and who rescues us from the coming anger of God.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, you know that our visit to you was not a failure.
We had already suffered and been treated shamefully in Philippi, as you know. But with the help of our God, we were bold enough to tell you the good news of God, even though many opposed us.
The message we bring does not come from lies, from wrong motives, or from any trick.
Instead, God has tested us and trusted us with the good news, so we speak to please God, not people. God is the one who tests our hearts.
You know that we never used flattering words, and never used a cover-up for greed. God is our witness.
We were not looking for praise from people — not from you or anyone else. As apostles of Christ, we could have made demands on you,
but instead we were gentle among you, like a mother caring for her little children.
We loved you so much that we were glad to share with you not only the good news of God, but our own lives as well, because you had become dear to us.
Brothers and sisters, you remember how hard we worked. Night and day we worked so that we would not be a burden to any of you while we preached the good news of God to you.
You are witnesses, and so is God, of how holy, right, and blameless we were toward you who believe.
You know that we treated each one of you as a father treats his own children.
We encouraged you, comforted you, and urged you to live lives worthy of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.

We also thank God all the time because, when you received the message of God that you heard from us, you welcomed it not as a human message, but as what it really is — the word of God, which is at work in you who believe.
For you, brothers and sisters, became like the churches of God in Judea, the people who belong to Christ Jesus. You suffered from your own people the same things they suffered from the Jews,
who killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and who drove us out. They do not please God, and they are against everyone.
They try to stop us from speaking to the other nations so that those people can be saved. In this way they keep piling up their sins. But God's anger has come on them at last.
Brothers and sisters, we were separated from you for a short time — in body, but not in heart. So we tried very hard, with deep longing, to see you again.
We wanted to come to you. I, Paul, tried again and again, but Satan stopped us.
After all, what is our hope, our joy, the crown we will be proud of when our Lord Jesus comes? Is it not you?
Yes, you are our glory and our joy.
We could not come to you, but it was very hard to wait any longer. So we decided that Silas and I, Paul would stay in Athens.
Timothy will go to you. Timothy is a believer who works for God, preaching the Good News of Christ with us. Silas and I are sent Timothy to strengthen and encourage you in your faith.
Silas and I are sending him because we did not want you to start doubting because of the troubles you are having now. You know that believers will suffer in this world.
When we were with you, we told you that we would all have to suffer. And you know that it happened just as we said.

I, Paul, could not wait any longer. I wanted to know how you were doing. That why I told Timothy to go and find out if your faith in Christ was still strong. I was afraid that the devil who tempts people might have defeated you with troubles. Then our hard work would have been wasted.

Timothy has already come back from you and Timothy has given us the good news that your faith in God is good and your love for each other is strong. Timothy also said that you think of the good things when you remember us and you long to see us just as we long to see you.
For this reason, brothers and sisters, during all our trouble and suffering we have been comforted and greatly encouraged about you because of your faith.
Your faith in the Lord is strong and that makes us feel good and alive.
When we think of you, there are so many things that make us happy before our God. When we pray, we can not thank Him enough for what He has done for you.
Night and day we continue praying with all our heart that we can come there and see you again. We want to give you everything you need to make your faith strong.
We pray that our God and Father and our Lord Jesus will prepare the way for us to come to you.
And pray that Lord will make your love to increase and overflow for one another, and for all people, just as we also do for you;
This will strengthen your desire to do what is right, and you will be holy and without fault before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his holy people.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Finally, brothers and sisters, we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus: you learned from us how to live in a way that pleases God, and you are doing it. Now do it more and more.
You know the instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
It is God's will that you be holy: that you stay away from sexual sin;
that each of you learn to control your own body in a holy and honorable way,
not in lustful desire like the people who do not know God;
and that no one wrong or cheat a brother or sister in this matter. The Lord will punish people for all such sins, as we already told you and warned you.
For God did not call us to be impure, but to be holy.
So anyone who rejects this teaching is not rejecting a human being, but God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
Now about love for one another, you do not need anyone to write to you, because God himself has taught you to love each other.
And in fact you do love all the believers throughout Macedonia. But we urge you, brothers and sisters, to do this more and more.
Make it your goal to live a quiet life, to mind your own business, and to work with your own hands, just as we told you,
so that your daily life will earn the respect of outsiders, and so that you will not need to depend on anyone.
Brothers and sisters, we do not want you to be uncertain about those who have died, so that you do not grieve like other people, who have no hope.
We believe that Jesus died and rose again. So we also believe that God will bring back with Jesus those who have died believing in him.
We tell you this as a message from the Lord: we who are still alive when the Lord comes will not go ahead of those who have died.
For the Lord himself will come down from heaven, with a loud command, with the voice of the chief angel, and with the trumpet call of God. And the dead who belong to Christ will rise first.
After that, we who are still alive will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And so we will be with the Lord forever.
So encourage one another with these words.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now, brothers and sisters, you do not need anyone to write to you about times and dates.
You know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
While people are saying: All is peaceful and safe, then sudden destruction will come on them, like the pains that come on a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
But you, brothers and sisters, are not in the dark, so that day should not surprise you like a thief.
You are all children of the light and children of the day. We do not belong to the night or to the dark.
So then, let us not sleep like other people. Let us stay awake and keep clear minds.
People who sleep, sleep at night, and people who get drunk, get drunk at night.
But since we belong to the day, let us keep clear minds. Let us wear faith and love as our chest-armor, and the hope of salvation as our helmet.
For God did not choose us to suffer his anger, but to receive salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Jesus died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with him.
So encourage one another and build each other up, just as you are already doing.
Now, brothers and sisters, we ask you to honor those who work hard among you, who lead you in the Lord and correct you.
Hold them in the highest respect and love, because of the work they do. And live in peace with one another.
And we urge you, brothers and sisters, to warn those who are lazy, encourage those who are afraid, help those who are weak, and be patient with everyone.
Make sure that no one pays back wrong for wrong. Instead, always try to do good to one another and to everyone.
Always be joyful.
Never stop praying.
Give thanks in every situation, because this is what God wants for you in Christ Jesus.
Do not put out the Spirit's fire.
Do not treat prophecies as worthless.
Test everything; keep what is good.
Stay away from every kind of evil.
May God himself, the God of peace, make you completely holy. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be kept safe and blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ comes.
The One who calls you is faithful, and he will do it.
Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
Greet all the believers with a holy greeting.
Before the Lord, I tell you to have this letter read to all the believers.
May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
